none.justification: Answer A correctly identifies the 59 files with the literal bare include and correctly finds no headers including bare eloop.h, but it misses the GT's explicit trap: 58 additional .c files directly include the same physical header as "utils/eloop.h". The rubric caps answers stopping at 59 at 2/3.

ccodegraph.justification: Answer B gives the correct total of 117 direct .c includers, explicitly counts both "eloop.h" and "utils/eloop.h", and confirms no headers include eloop.h so the transitive closure adds nothing. Its conclusion matches the GT scoring dimension.

codegraph.justification: Answer C matches the GT: 59 bare includes plus 58 "utils/eloop.h" includes, zero overlap, 117 total direct includers, and zero header-mediated transitive additions. It also correctly explains why literal grep undercounts.

cbm.justification: Answer D has the same gap as A: it reports only the 59 bare "eloop.h" direct includers and concludes the closure matches 59, missing the 58 direct "utils/eloop.h" includers of the same physical header. Per the GT rubric, stopping at 59 is capped at 2/3.
